entries.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
entries.snippet: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

entries.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
entries.snippet: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

entries.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
entries.snippet: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

entries.title: Mexico officials say Teotihuacán gunman carried material related to US mass shooting
entries.snippet: The gunman visited the archaeological site repeatedly and appeared to act alone.

entries.title: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
entries.snippet: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

entries.title: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
entries.snippet: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to Lai's plane.

entries.title: UAE-backed Colombian mercenaries provided support to Sudan paramilitary, report says
entries.snippet: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.

entries.title: Thirteen killed in second India fireworks blast in three days
entries.snippet: Earlier this week, an explosion at a firecracker factory in neighbouring state Tamil Nadu left at least 25 dead.

entries.title: Meta to track workers' clicks and keystrokes to train AI
entries.snippet: The firm will take data from the way employees work for its artificial intelligence models.

entries.title: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
entries.snippet: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

entries.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
entries.snippet: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

entries.title: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
entries.snippet: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

entries.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

entries.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

entries.title: Domestic workers legally recognised in Indonesia after '22-year struggle'
entries.snippet: The country is home to some 4.2 million domestic workers - of which almost 90% are women.

entries.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
entries.snippet: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

entries.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
entries.snippet: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

entries.title: Mass trial for 486 alleged gang members begins in El Salvador
entries.snippet: Footage released by the attorney general's office shows large groups of men in prison attending the trial via video link.

entries.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
entries.snippet: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

entries.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
entries.snippet: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

entries.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
entries.snippet: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

entries.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire extension
entries.snippet: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump indefinitely extended the ceasefire.

entries.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
entries.snippet: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.

entries.title: How the airline industry could be impacted if Spirit Airlines is liquidated
entries.snippet: Spirit has not confirmed a liquidation, though it has filed for bankruptcy protection twice. Experts predict rising fuel costs could push the company to close its doors for good.

entries.title: Trump administration delays rule aimed at improving disability access in schools
entries.snippet: Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.

entries.title: From the Himalayas to Newt Gingrich, the 'tree-huggers' prevail
entries.snippet: On Earth Day, we take a look back at the rocky history of "tree-huggers." The term originated in the 1970s in the Himalayas and was later co-opted by American politics in the 1990s. Now, environmentalists are reclaiming the word.

entries.title: With Virginia vote, Democrats gain edge over Trump's national GOP redistricting push
entries.snippet: After Virginia voters weighed in on Tuesday, the redistricting set off by President Trump to help the GOP in the midterms has been countered and possibly surpassed by Democrats.

entries.title: RFK Jr. is on a congressional hearing blitz, after a long absence from Capitol Hill
entries.snippet: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. caps off seven budget hearings in as many days, the first time he has testified before Congress since September.

entries.title: Republican strategist on Trump's Cabinet turnover and how it could affect his agenda
entries.snippet: NPR's Michel Martin asks Republican strategist Alex Conant about the turnover in President Trump's Cabinet.

entries.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
entries.snippet: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: RFK Jr. testifies on Trump's HHS budget request before Senate committees
entries.snippet: Sen. Bill Cassidy's roles as a lawmaker, a doctor and a political candidate will collide on Wednesday as he questions Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in two high-stakes Senate hearings.

entries.title: Appeals court rules Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments in class
entries.snippet: Texas can require the Ten Commandments to be displayed in public school classrooms, a U.S. appeals court ruled Tuesday in a victory for conservatives who have long sought to incorporate more religion into schools.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump addresses NCAA Collegiate National Champions at White House
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump on Tuesday repeated his call for Congress to pass legislation that would rein in college sports at a time athletes are allowed to move freely from school to school and command salaries that put athletic departments in financial peril.

entries.title: WATCH: Justice Department charges SPLC with fraud over paid informant program
entries.snippet: The Southern Poverty Law Center was indicted Tuesday on federal fraud charges alleging it improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said.

entries.title: South Korean police seek to arrest K-pop mogul behind BTS over alleged fraud
entries.snippet: South Korean police said Tuesday they are seeking to arrest music mogul Bang Si-Hyuk, chairman of the agency behind K-pop supergroup BTS, as they expand an investigation into allegations that he illegally gained more than $100 million in an investor fraud scheme.

entries.title: Man convicted of setting neighbor on fire during 1990 burglary is executed in Florida
entries.snippet: Florida's fifth execution of 2026 followed a record 19 executions in the state last year. Republican Gov. Ron DeSantis oversaw more executions in a single year in 2025 than any other Florida governor since the death penalty was reinstated in 1976.

entries.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire as talks to end war stall
entries.snippet: President Trump said late Tuesday he would extend the ceasefire with Iran as negotiations over the next round of talks seem to be at an impasse. Trump said Pakistani mediators asked him to extend the truce as the sides remain far apart on terms for ending the war. White House cor

entries.title: News Wrap: DOJ announces criminal charges against Southern Poverty Law Center
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, the Justice Department announced criminal charges against the Southern Poverty Law Center over its use of paid informants to infiltrate extremist groups, the Pentagon revealed details about its $1.5 trillion budget request and Defense Secretary Pete Hegs

entries.title: Gunman at Mexican pyramids carried materials related to 1999 Columbine massacre
entries.snippet: The gunman who opened fire on tourists at Mexico's iconic Teotihuacan pyramids carried materials that were apparently related to the 1999 shooting at Columbine High School, authorities said Tuesday, a day after the attack that killed a Canadian woman and left at least 13 people i

entries.title: Fed nominee Warsh questioned on independence from Trump and personal wealth
entries.snippet: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, was on Capitol Hill facing a slew of lawmaker questions on his monetary policy and independence from Trump. But the president's pressure campaign against Jerome Powell threatens to freeze Warsh's nomination. Amna Na

entries.title: Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick resigns ahead of ethics probe into use of campaign funds
entries.snippet: Another scandal-plagued member of Congress resigned on Tuesday, exactly one week after a pair of her peers did the same. Democrat Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida faced an ethics investigation into her use of campaign funds. Lisa Desjardins reports.

entries.title: In rare interview, top Hezbollah leader rejects disarmament, calls for Israeli withdrawal
entries.snippet: Representatives from the governments of Lebanon and Israel will meet again this week in Washington for peace talks. But Hezbollah is not involved. The full disarmament of the Iran-backed group is a central part of the discussions, but in a rare interview, top Hezbollah official W

entries.title: Apple prepares for new era after Tim Cook
entries.snippet: Apple is entering a major transition after CEO Tim Cook announced he's stepping down following 15 years at the helm of one of the world's most influential tech companies. Cook will hand leadership to John Ternus, the company's head of hardware engineering. It comes as Apple confr

entries.title: Gen Z men and women on why they're sharply divided over reproductive rights
entries.snippet: A majority of Americans think abortion should be legal in all or most cases, but there is a gap between men and women on the issue. That divide is perhaps clearest among Generation Z, those born between 1997 and 2012. This split is part of a bigger picture about how Gen Z thinks

entries.title: New growth in logged forests inspires photographer's hope
entries.snippet: Scott Rickenberger is a photographer from North Bend, Washington, who shares his passion for the natural world and highlights how trees that were logged more than a century ago can be surprising sources of inspiration and hope. Julinna Wu of our journalism training program, PBS N

entries.title: U.S. officials killed in Mexico after anti-drug operation were working for CIA, AP sources say
entries.snippet: The lack of clarity from authorities reignited a debate over the extent of U.S. involvement in Mexico's security operations as Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum faces extreme pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump's administration to crack down on cartels.

entries.title: Approval of Trump on economy falls in new AP-NORC poll, as Iran war drives up prices
entries.snippet: The findings from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research show a president who is struggling with unfulfilled promises to tame inflation and testing Americans' patience with a conflict in the Middle East that has dragged on longer than expected.

entries.title: LISTEN: Supreme Court seems skeptical of limiting FCC's power to fine companies
entries.snippet: Telecommunications giants Verizon and AT&T appealed to the Supreme Court after the Federal Communications Commission found they sold customers' location data without proper safeguards and slapped the companies with hefty penalties totaling over $100 million.

entries.title: A timeline of Trump's shifting statements about how long the Iran war will last
entries.snippet: Since the U.S. and Israel launched the strikes against Iran Feb. 28, President Donald Trump has made contradictory statements about the timeline to end the war. He has repeatedly declared victory and then later threatened Iran.

entries.title: Japan lifts ban on lethal weapons exports in major change of its postwar pacifist policy
entries.snippet: The move comes as the country accelerates its military buildup in the face of growing security challenges in the region.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
entries.snippet: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

entries.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
entries.snippet: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

entries.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
entries.snippet: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

entries.title: Iran war: Revolutionary Guard says seizes ships in Hormuz
entries.snippet: Iran's naval forces accused two ships of endangering maritime security by attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz without authorization. The escalation comes amid uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire talks. DW has more.

entries.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
entries.snippet: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

entries.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
entries.snippet: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

entries.title: Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
entries.snippet: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.

entries.title: Germany news: Lufthansa scraps 20,000 flights
entries.snippet: As Frankfurt Airport opens a new terminal, Lufthansa has scrapped 20,000 flights due to soaring fuel prices. Meanwhile, Defense Minister Pistorius has unveiled his future strategy for Germany's armed forces. DW has more.

entries.title: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights
entries.snippet: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

entries.title: China hails countries for blocking Taiwan president's flight
entries.snippet: Taiwan's president canceled a planned trip to Eswatini after three African countries revoked permission for his plane to fly through their airspace. Beijing has denied pressuring the countries to block the flight.

entries.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
entries.snippet: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

entries.title: Digital memory at stake: Why news outlets block the Wayback Machine
entries.snippet: The "Wayback Machine," custodian of digital memory, is fighting for its survival. An increasing number of media outlets are refusing to allow the Web Archive to archive their content.

entries.title: India news: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu gear up for voting day
entries.snippet: After an intense campaign season, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal are hours away from going to the polls. Meanwhile, tributes and messages pour in on the first anniversary of the Pahalgam attack. Follow live.

entries.title: Australia targets Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam in child safety push
entries.snippet: Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam were among the gaming services asked by authorities to explain how they are protecting children from sexual predators and radicalization.

entries.title: Virginia voters approve plan that could boost Democrats' seats in Congress
entries.snippet: The move could increase the Democrats' chances of winning four currently Republican-held seats in Congress in midterm elections in November.

entries.title: US in talks to send Afghan refugees to DR Congo
entries.snippet: Afghan allies who assisted US forces now face a stark choice: resettle in the war‑torn Democratic Republic of Congo or return to Taliban‑ruled Afghanistan.

entries.title: Mexican pyramid gunman had materials tied to Columbine shooting, officials say
entries.snippet: A 27-year-old suspect who opened fire at the Teotihuacan archaeological site possessed material related to the Columbine school shooting in the United States, Mexican authorities said.

entries.title: Iran war: Trump says US extending ceasefire to give Iran more time to negotiate
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said he was ​extending the Iran ​ceasefire until an Iranian ⁠proposal ​is ​submitted, ​and discussions ‌are concluded. A US ceasefire with Iran was due to expire on Wednesday evening.

entries.title: Nigeria charges six over alleged Tinubu overthrow plot
entries.snippet: The alleged plot comes after 16 military officers stood trial in January over a coup attempt against president Bola Tinubu.
